arts.ttl: Davide Nicola questions Juventus penalty decision: “How can Luperto forget he has arms while falling?”
arts.sum: Cagliari manager Davide Nicola has expressed his disagreement with the penalty awarded to Juventus in their recent 1-1 draw. While not contesting the technical aspect of the decision, Nicola raised concerns about the interpretation of the handball rule. “From a technical point of view, I’m not convinced by the decision to award such a penalty,” Nicola stated in an interview with DAZN. He acknowledged that Luperto’s touch was objective, albeit marginal. Nicola’s main contention lies with the rule’s interpretation and its application in dynamic situations. He explained, “If I’m contesting an aerial duel with Vlahovic or Gatti, it’s obvious that The post Davide Nicola questions Juventus penalty decision: “How can Luperto forget he has arms while falling?” appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Yerry Mina: ‘I made a gesture at the Juve fans – I didn’t like how Cuadrado was treated by the club’
arts.sum: Cagliari defender Yerry Mina expressed his satisfaction with his team’s performance following their 1-1 draw against Juventus. Speaking to DAZN after the match, Mina highlighted the importance of the point earned and addressed several aspects of the game. “Vlahovic is very good,” Mina acknowledged, praising the Juventus striker’s abilities. Despite the intense on-field competition, the Colombian defender emphasized that any confrontations during the match stay on the pitch. Celebrating the result, Mina stated, “Now we’re going home to celebrate an important point.” Mina also discussed his vocal presence on the field, explaining, “It’s important to always talk on the pitch The post Yerry Mina: ‘I made a gesture at the Juve fans – I didn’t like how Cuadrado was treated by the club’ appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Juventus Player Ratings vs Cagliari: Douglas Luiz Impedes the Old Lady Again
arts.sum: Despite getting an early lead from the spot, the wasteful Bianconeri endured a torrid ending as Douglas Luiz gave away a penalty that allowed Razvan Marin to equalize, while Francisco Conceicao left the hosts with a man down. So here are our player ratings for Juventus who could only pick up a single point from their encounter against Cagliari. arts.ttl: Thiago Motta Blasts Juventus Approach After Taking Lead Against Cagliari
arts.sum: Serie A title hopefuls Juventus dropped two valuable points in a stomach-wrenching 1-1 home draw against relegation-battling Cagliari on Sunday. Thiago Motta addressed the media after the game, delivering his verdict on a morale-wracking result at the Allianz Stadium. arts.ttl: Thiago Motta: “We managed instead of attacking. Vlahovic? It happens to everyone.”
arts.sum: Juventus manager Thiago Motta expressed his disappointment following his team’s 1-1 draw against Cagliari, highlighting missed opportunities and a lack of attacking intent. The Bianconeri led for most of the match but conceded a late equalizer, marking their first goal allowed in Serie A this season. Motta began by analyzing the team’s performance: “We controlled the game in the first half, but we managed instead of attacking. In the second half, we created chances but didn’t finish them. There was always a feeling that Cagliari could get back into the game. We did almost everything ourselves.” Addressing Dusan Vlahovic’s missed The post Thiago Motta: “We managed instead of attacking. Vlahovic? It happens to everyone.” appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Juventus held by Cagliari in front of watching Paul Pogba
arts.sum: To add insult to injury, Juventus winger Francisco Conceição was sent off moments later following a second yellow card. Less than a minute later Conceição went down in the penalty area after feeling Adam Obert’s hand on his shoulder.
